summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Timer Trigger) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists the results to Azure Blob Storage, using Managed Identity exclusively (no API keys).
assumptions: TRAPI refers to an internal Azure/Microsoft proxy service for GPT-4o access that supports Managed Identity authentication.
Azure Functions Consumption or Flex Consumption plan is acceptable for this low-frequency nightly workload.
Python is the preferred implementation language given the simplicity of the workload.
A single Azure Storage Blob container is sufficient for output; each nightly run overwrites or appends a dated blob.
IaC will use Bicep and deployment via GitHub Actions.
The target Azure subscription and resource group will be provided by the team; this plan does not assume greenfield subscription creation.
No VNet integration is required unless the TRAPI endpoint mandates it.
The nightly schedule is interpreted as 00:00 UTC unless specified otherwise.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and required request schema for GPT-4o access?
Which Azure subscription and resource group should host the Function App and Storage Account?
Should each nightly run overwrite a single blob or create a new dated blob per run?
What RBAC roles does the Function App Managed Identity need on the TRAPI service principal/scope?
Is there a required blob container name or naming convention for the output?
What is the exact nightly schedule time (timezone/UTC)?
Are there data retention requirements for stored blobs?
Should failed runs trigger alerts, and if so, to what notification channel?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish project governance, infrastructure scaffolding, CI/CD pipeline, and identity baseline so all subsequent build work has a deployable target.
entry_criteria: Target Azure subscription and resource group identified
exit_criteria: Azure Function App and Storage Account provisioned in target environment
Managed Identity created and assigned to Function App
CI/CD pipeline deploys successfully to the Function App

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement and deploy the three functional requirements: TRAPI/GPT-4o query, Blob Storage write, and nightly Timer Trigger.
entry_criteria: TRAPI endpoint URL and Managed Identity RBAC role confirmed
Blob container name and naming convention confirmed
exit_criteria: Timer Trigger fires on schedule in dev/staging environment
TRAPI call returns 1985 Yankees roster successfully
Roster blob written to Storage Account without API keys
No secrets or API keys present in code or configuration

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, error handling, observability, and test coverage to production-harden the function.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
Function executes end-to-end successfully at least once
exit_criteria: Retry and error handling logic implemented and tested
Structured logging and Application Insights connected
Alert rule for failed runs configured
Unit and integration tests pass in CI

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate production deployment, confirm nightly schedule, verify monitoring, and complete acceptance sign-off.
entry_criteria: All acceptance criteria from spec.md verified in staging
exit_criteria: Nightly Timer Trigger confirmed firing in production
Blob output verified in production Storage Account
Monitoring and alerting active in production

tasks.title: [Task PM-01] Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Owner (rciapala) reviews this task plan, resolves open questions, and formally approves it before implementation begins. Confirm TRAPI endpoint details, target subscription, blob naming convention, and schedule time.
acceptance_criteria: All open questions have documented answers
Task plan approved by rciapala
Target Azure subscription and resource group confirmed
TRAPI endpoint URL and auth scope confirmed
Nightly schedule time (UTC) confirmed
risks: TRAPI endpoint details unavailable, blocking APP-02
Subscription/resource group not yet provisioned, blocking INF-01

tasks.title: Provision Azure Function App and App Service Plan via Bicep
tasks.description: Author Bicep templates to provision a Consumption or Flex Consumption Azure Function App with an associated storage account for the Functions runtime, deploying to the confirmed resource group. Enable system-assigned Managed Identity on the Function App.
inputs: Confirmed Azure subscription and resource group
outputs: Deployed Function App with Managed Identity enabled
Function App resource ID and Managed Identity principal ID
acceptance_criteria: Bicep template is idempotent and passes `az bicep build` without errors
Function App deployed to target resource group
System-assigned Managed Identity is enabled on the Function App
Function App runtime is Python 3.11+
No secrets or connection strings stored in App Settings beyond system-required values
risks: Subscription quota limits on Consumption plan in target region
TRAPI may require VNet integration not currently planned

tasks.title: Provision Azure Blob Storage Account for player data output
tasks.description: Extend Bicep templates to provision an Azure Storage Account and Blob container for storing nightly Yankees roster output. Configure container name per confirmed naming convention.
inputs: Confirmed blob container name and naming convention
outputs: Bicep template for Storage Account and container
acceptance_criteria: Storage Account deployed with LRS redundancy minimum
Blob container created with confirmed name
Public blob access disabled
Storage Account accessible via Managed Identity (no shared key auth required by Function)
risks: Storage Account name collisions (globally unique requirement)

tasks.title: Assign RBAC roles to Function App Managed Identity
tasks.description: Assign the Storage Blob Data Contributor role to the Function App Managed Identity on the output Storage Account. Assign the required TRAPI RBAC role/scope to the Managed Identity so the function can authenticate to TRAPI without API keys.
inputs: Function App Managed Identity principal ID (from INF-01)
TRAPI required RBAC scope and role (from PM-01)
outputs: Verified Managed Identity access to Storage and TRAPI
acceptance_criteria: Storage Blob Data Contributor assigned to Function App MI on the output container
TRAPI RBAC role assigned to Function App MI
Role assignments codified in Bicep (not applied manually)
Verified: Function App MI can obtain a token for TRAPI scope
risks: TRAPI RBAC role name/scope unknown until PM-01 resolved
Propagation delay in AAD role assignments may affect initial testing

tasks.title: Configure GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Function App
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that lints, runs tests, packages the Python function app, and deploys to the Azure Function App using federated identity (OIDC) — no stored credentials.
inputs: Function App name and resource group (from INF-01)
outputs: Successful deployment run to staging/production slot
acceptance_criteria: Workflow triggers on push to main branch
Workflow runs lint (flake8 or ruff) and unit tests before deploy
Deployment uses OIDC federated identity (no stored Azure credentials in GitHub secrets)
Successful deployment verified by checking Function App status post-deploy
Failed deployments cause workflow to exit non-zero
risks: OIDC federated identity setup requires Azure AD app registration permissions
Function App cold-start after deployment may cause initial Timer Trigger miss

tasks.title: Implement Azure Timer Trigger function with nightly CRON schedule
tasks.description: Scaffold the Azure Functions Python project and implement a Timer Trigger function configured to execute nightly at the confirmed UTC time. Wire up the application entry point to orchestrate the TRAPI call and blob write.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
outputs: Python Azure Function project with Timer Trigger
acceptance_criteria: Timer Trigger CRON expression matches confirmed nightly schedule
Function app starts locally with `func start` without errors
Timer fires on schedule when deployed to Function App
Function execution logged with run timestamp
risks: Incorrect CRON expression causing missed or double-firing runs

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o client to retrieve 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to TRAPI using the Function App Managed Identity (azure-identity DefaultAzureCredential), calls GPT-4o with a prompt to list all members of the 1985 New York Yankees, and returns a structured list of player names.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and request schema (from PM-01)
acceptance_criteria: Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential — no API keys or connection strings in code
GPT-4o prompt returns a parseable list of 1985 Yankees roster members
Response parsed into a structured Python list/dict
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP call and validate parsing logic
HTTP errors and empty responses raise explicit exceptions
risks: GPT-4o response format may vary; prompt engineering required for consistent structured output
TRAPI endpoint details unknown until PM-01 resolved — blocks implementation
LLM hallucination risk: roster may be incomplete or contain errors

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer for roster output
tasks.description: Implement a Python module that authenticates to Azure Blob Storage using DefaultAzureCredential and writes the structured Yankees roster to the output container as a JSON blob. Each run should produce a dated blob (e.g., 1985-yankees-YYYYMMDD.json) or overwrite a fixed blob per confirmed convention.
inputs: Storage Account name and container name (from INF-02)
acceptance_criteria: Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential — no storage account keys or SAS tokens in code
Blob written to confirmed container with confirmed naming convention
Blob content is valid JSON containing the full player list
Unit tests mock the BlobServiceClient and validate write logic
Storage errors raise explicit exceptions caught by the Timer Trigger handler
risks: Blob overwrite vs. append strategy not confirmed — may require re-work if convention changes

tasks.title: Security review: validate no API keys, secrets, or credentials in code or config
tasks.description: Perform a security review of the codebase and GitHub Actions workflow to confirm no API keys, storage account keys, SAS tokens, or other secrets are present in source code, environment variables, or repository secrets. Validate Managed Identity is the sole authentication mechanism.
acceptance_criteria: No API keys, passwords, or connection strings found in source code via secret scanning
GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC — no stored Azure credentials in repository secrets
Azure Function App Settings contain no secrets beyond system-required values
Storage Account shared key access disabled or not used by the Function
Bicep templates reviewed: no hard-coded credentials
risks: Developer inadvertently commits a TRAPI token during local testing

tasks.title: Implement retry logic and error handling for TRAPI and Blob Storage failures
tasks.description: Add retry logic (exponential backoff) to TRAPI HTTP calls and Blob Storage writes. Implement a top-level exception handler in the Timer Trigger that catches unhandled errors, logs structured error details, and does not silently swallow failures.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry logic
Tests for retry and failure scenarios
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI HTTP calls retry up to 3 times with exponential backoff on transient errors
Blob write retries on transient storage errors
Unhandled exceptions logged with structured error context before re-raising
Failed function execution visible in Application Insights as a failed invocation
Tests simulate TRAPI and Storage transient failures and verify retry behavior
risks: Overly aggressive retry on TRAPI may exhaust token quota

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights and structured logging
tasks.description: Connect the Azure Function App to an Application Insights instance. Implement structured logging throughout the application (TRAPI call, response parsing, blob write). Configure an alert rule for failed function invocations to notify the team.
inputs: Application Insights workspace or new instance
outputs: Application Insights connected to Function App
Structured log statements in all modules
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights resource provisioned and connected to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting
Each function invocation produces a structured log entry with run timestamp and outcome
TRAPI call duration and HTTP status logged
Blob write success/failure logged
Alert rule fires when function invocation failure rate exceeds 1 in a 24-hour window
Alert notification reaches confirmed channel
risks: Alert notification channel not confirmed until PM-01 resolved

tasks.title: Write and execute integration tests for end-to-end function execution
tasks.description: Write integration tests that execute the full function logic against a staging Storage Account (using real Managed Identity credentials in CI) and validate that a blob is written with expected content. Include a test that validates the 1985 Yankees roster is non-empty and contains known players.
inputs: Staging Storage Account and Function App
Known 1985 Yankees player list for assertion
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests run in GitHub Actions CI against staging environment
Test asserts blob is created in Storage container after function execution
Test asserts blob JSON contains at least N known 1985 Yankees players (e.g., Don Mattingly, Rickey Henderson)
Test asserts no API keys are used in the execution path
All tests pass in CI before merge to main
risks: TRAPI availability in CI environment may require a mock/stub for unit pipeline runs
LLM non-determinism may cause flaky roster completeness assertions

tasks.title: Deploy to production and verify nightly Timer Trigger
tasks.description: Execute production deployment via CI/CD pipeline. Verify the Timer Trigger fires at the configured nightly schedule, the TRAPI call succeeds, and the roster blob is written to the production Storage Account. Obtain owner sign-off.
outputs: Verified nightly blob output in production
acceptance_criteria: Production deployment succeeds via CI/CD pipeline without manual steps
Timer Trigger fires at confirmed nightly schedule time in production
Blob written to production Storage container with correct content
Application Insights shows successful invocation
No API keys present in production configuration
rciapala provides written sign-off
risks: TRAPI endpoint not available in production environment
Managed Identity RBAC propagation delay causing first-run failure

tasks.title: Execute production acceptance validation and complete success criteria checklist
tasks.description: Walk through the spec.md success criteria checklist against the production deployment. Verify all functional requirements, acceptance criteria, code review, test coverage, monitoring, and documentation are complete.
outputs: Any outstanding gaps resolved or documented
acceptance_criteria: All spec.md functional requirement acceptance criteria checked off
Nightly execution confirmed over at least one real nightly cycle
Monitoring and alerting confirmed active
Code review completed and merged
Test coverage meets agreed threshold
rciapala confirms project complete
risks: Waiting for a real nightly cycle adds 24-hour minimum to release readiness
